name: kv_dense_v1
goal: Measure whether Apple-native prefix reuse plus bounded or paged KV can beat the stock path on warm TTFT, shared-prefix concurrency, memory-bounded stability, and long-context behavior.
charter: Follow bench/BENCHMARK_CHARTER.md. Do not publish best-of-N alone. Prefer median and p95. Treat tiny-model single-stream wins as insufficient for repo-level claims.

backends.name: mlx_lm
backends.notes: Primary internal baseline and default comparison target.

backends.name: siliconrt_candidate
backends.notes: Candidate runtime or cache policy under test.

backends.name: llama_cpp_metal
backends.notes: External Apple baseline when the same model family is available.

backends.name: vllm_metal
backends.notes: Compare only when the same workload is reproducible cleanly; do not force apples-to-oranges comparisons.

model_slots.name: dense_debug_small
model_slots.notes: Fast iteration model for harness bring-up and bug fixing.

model_slots.name: dense_main
model_slots.notes: Primary truth-terrain model for KV-first work.

model_slots.name: dense_large_optional
model_slots.notes: Only after the harness is stable and the machine budget is clear.

workloads.name: cold_vs_warm_prefix
workloads.purpose: Measure warm-prefix TTFT reduction on a single request.
workloads.notes: Same large prefix, varying suffixes, repeated several times.

workloads.name: shared_prefix_burst
workloads.purpose: Measure latency and throughput under shared-prefix concurrency.
workloads.notes: Burst several requests with the same prefix and distinct suffixes.

workloads.name: bounded_memory_pressure
workloads.purpose: Measure eviction and stability under a fixed memory budget.
workloads.notes: Many distinct prefixes; force reuse, eviction, and promotion behavior.

workloads.name: long_multi_turn
workloads.purpose: Measure later-turn behavior as history grows.
workloads.notes: Growing history across 8-20 turns per session.

workloads.name: mixed_hits_misses
workloads.purpose: Measure cache usefulness without hiding cold-start penalties.
workloads.notes: Use a realistic hit-rate sweep instead of only all-hit workloads.

metrics.name: ttft_p99_ms
metrics.notes: Very-tail TTFT when sample size allows.

metrics.name: max_concurrent_sessions_before_failure
metrics.notes: Capacity under the tested memory and cache policy.

metrics.name: cache_hit_rate
metrics.notes: Observed hit rate, not only target hit rate.

metrics.name: eviction_count
metrics.notes: How often cache entries were evicted.

comparison_rules: Use the same model family, quantization, hardware, and prompt matrix whenever possible.
Treat siliconrt vs mlx_lm as the primary A/B gate.
Treat llama.cpp Metal and vllm-metal as external references, not as excuses for apples-to-oranges comparisons.
Treat public MetalRT numbers as published external references unless the setup is actually reproduced.
Mark any non-equivalent comparison explicitly in the result note.
reporting_rules: Record median results for repeated runs.
Record ttft_p95_ms when sample count allows.
Never report best-of-N as the only headline metric.
A serious repo-level claim should include at least one dense 7B-8B class 4-bit model when the machine budget allows.
Record explicit byte budget and eviction behavior for bounded-memory experiments.
